system_prompt: You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to review the provided error logs and diagnose the underlying issues. You will be provided with a similar case reference, which is a list of similar cases that are ordered by similarity. You can use this reference to help you understand the user requirement and the error.When an error indicates that a specific keyword is undefined (for example, 'div(phi,(p|rho)) is undefined'), your response must propose a solution that simply defines that exact keyword as shown in the error log. Do not reinterpret or modify the keyword (e.g., do not treat '|' as 'or'); instead, assume it is meant to be taken literally. Propose ideas on how to resolve the errors, but do not modify any files directly. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. Do not ask the user any questions.The user will supply all relevant foam files along with the error logs, and within the logs, you will find both the error content and the corresponding error command indicated by the log file name.

<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.3;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.01;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

relaxationFactors
{
    equations
    {
        ".*"            1;
    }
}

<Review_Analysis>
## Error Diagnosis

The error is clear:

```
keyword div(phi,epsilon) is undefined in dictionary ".../system/fvSchemes/divSchemes"
```

The `pimpleFoam` solver requires a divergence scheme for `div(phi,epsilon)`, but the current `fvSchemes` file only defines:
- `div(phi,U)`
- `div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U)))))`

It does **not** define `div(phi,epsilon)` or a fallback `default` entry in the `divSchemes` sub-dictionary.

## Root Cause

In the `pitzDaily` tutorial (which this case is based on), the `divSchemes` section includes entries for all scalar transport fields used by `pimpleFoam`, including `div(phi,epsilon)`. Since `epsilon` is being transported (as part of the k-epsilon turbulence model), the solver needs to know how to compute its convective term.

## Proposed Solution

Add the missing divergence scheme entry to the `divSchemes` sub-dictionary in **`system/fvSchemes`**:

```
divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss linear;
}
```

This adds exactly what the error requests: the `div(phi,epsilon)` keyword with a `Gauss linear` scheme. This follows the pattern established in the reference tutorial case where all scalar field divergences are explicitly listed. No other changes should be needed since the rest of the configuration (PIMPLE settings, boundary conditions, properties) already aligns with the user requirements.
</Review_Analysis>

Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 15m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls. Use timestep of 0.0001 and output every 0.01. Finaltime is 0.3. use nu value of 1e-04.</user_requirement>

I have modified the files according to your previous suggestions. If the error persists, please provide further guidance. Make sure your suggestions adhere to user requirements and do not contradict it. Also, please consider the previous attempts and try a different approach.
